text:
SITUATION: www.speakingbrazilian.com
PHRASES & USAGE:
- PT: queridos alunos! | EN: Hello, dear students!

SITUATION: 1. How to greet someone in Portuguese
PHRASES & USAGE:
- PT: Oi! | EN: Hi! [Formal]
- PT: Olá! | EN: Hello! [Formal]
- PT: Tudo bem? | EN: How are you? [Formal]
- PT: Tudo bom? | EN: How are you? [Formal]
- PT: Bom dia! | EN: Good morning! [Formal]
- PT: Boa tarde! | EN: Good afternoon! [Formal]
- PT: Boa noite! | EN: Good evening! [Formal]
- PT: beleza? | EN: So, how’s it going? [Formal]
- PT: Opa! | EN: Hey! [Formal]
- PT: Fala aí! | EN: What’s up! [Formal]
- PT: Tchau! | EN: Bye! [Formal]
- PT: Até logo! | EN: See you soon! [Formal]
- PT: Até breve! | EN: See you soon! [Formal]
- PT: Até mais tarde! | EN: See you later! [Formal]
- PT: Até a próxima! | EN: Until next time! [Formal]
- PT: Bom dia! | EN: Good morning! [Formal]
- PT: Boa tarde! | EN: Good afternoon! [Formal]
- PT: Boa noite! | EN: Good night! [Formal]
- PT: Tenha um bom dia! | EN: Have a good day! [Formal]
- PT: Tenha uma boa tarde! | EN: Have a good afternoon! [Formal]
- PT: Tenha uma boa noite! | EN: Have a good night! [Formal]

metadata.topic: 1. How to greet someone in Portuguese
metadata.author: Virginia Magalhães

text:
SITUATION: 2. How to introduce yourself in Portuguese
PHRASES & USAGE:
- PT:  | EN: Hi! I’m Virginia. [Formal]
- PT:  | EN: My name is Virginia. [Formal]
- PT: Qual é o seu nome? | EN: What’s your name? [Formal]
- PT:  | EN: My name is Virginia. [Formal]
- PT: Como você se chama? | EN: What is your name? [Formal]
- PT: Prazer! | EN: Nice to meet you! [Formal]
- PT: Muito prazer! | EN: Very nice to meet you! [Formal]
- PT: O prazer é meu! | EN: The pleasure is mine! [Formal]
- PT: lo! | EN: It’s nice to meet you! [Formal]
- PT: la! | EN: It’s nice to meet you! [Formal]

metadata.topic: 2. How to introduce yourself in Portuguese
metadata.author: Virginia Magalhães

text:
SITUATION: 3. How to communicate when you don’t understand something,
PHRASES & USAGE:
- PT: em português? | EN: How do you say… in Portuguese?
- PT: Como se escreve? | EN: How do you write?
- PT: Como se soletra? | EN: How do you spell?
- PT: ? | EN: What does… mean?
- PT:  | EN: I understand.
- PT:  | EN: I’m sorry, I don’t understand.
- PT: por favor? | EN: Could you repeat that, please?
- PT: por favor? | EN: Could you speak slower, please?
- PT:  | EN: I’m sorry, I don’t speak Portuguese.
- PT: Você fala inglês? | EN: Do you speak English?

metadata.topic: 3. How to communicate when you don’t understand something,
metadata.author: Virginia Magalhães

text:
SITUATION: 4. Words and phrases that you should use to be polite.
PHRASES & USAGE:
- PT: Por favor | EN: Please [Formal]
- PT: Obrigada | EN: Thank you [Formal]
- PT: Brigado | EN: Thanks [Formal]
- PT: Brigada | EN: Thanks [Formal]
- PT:  | EN: thanks [Formal]
- PT: De nada | EN: You’re welcome [Formal]
- PT: Por nada | EN: You’re welcome [Formal]
- PT: Não há de quê | EN: You’re welcome [Formal]
- PT:  | EN: No worries! [Formal]
- PT: Desculpa | EN: I’m sorry [Formal]
- PT: Com licença | EN: Excuse me [Formal]
- PT: or simply 
Licença | EN: Excuse me [Formal]

metadata.topic: 4. Words and phrases that you should use to be polite.
metadata.author: Virginia Magalhães

text:
SITUATION: 5. How to ask for information if you are a little lost and need
PHRASES & USAGE:
- PT: Estou perdida | EN: o
- PT:  | EN: Excuse me. I’m lost. I need help.
- PT:  | EN: I’m looking for the beach/ the 
supermarket/ the pharmacy.
- PT: ? | EN: Do you know where hotel… is?
- PT: à praia? | EN: How do I get to the museum / the beach?
- PT: Você sabe onde tem um caixa eletrônico? | EN: Do you know where there's an ATM?
- PT: Você sabe onde tem uma estação de metrô ou ônibus por aqui? | EN: Do you know where 
there is a subway or bus station close by?
- PT: ao hotel? | EN: How do I get to… the beach/ the 
supermarket/ the hotel?
- PT: o museu? | EN: Do you know where the… beach/ hotel/ 
museum is?
- PT: uma farmácia? | EN: Do you 
know where there is… an ATM, a taxi stop, a pharmacy?

metadata.topic: 5. How to ask for information if you are a little lost and need
metadata.author: Virginia Magalhães
